persons.arguments_against: The bio carries a single initial and no birth year, and the corpus holds 5 staff-list officials with this surname, so the namesake trap is live: the staff-list 'J. Harward' could be a different Harward, e.g. a relative who also taught in Ceylon. This fails because the stint reproduces the bio's entire three-stage career with the right dates, not just one office: 'Principal of the Royal College' from 1894 (bio: appointed 1st Apr. 1892), 'Director' of the 'Department of Public Instruction' from 1905 (bio: dir. of pub. instrn. 23rd June 1903), and the department label switching to 'Education Department' precisely in the 1914 edition (bio: 'designation of office changed to dir. of educn., Aug., 1913'). Principal of the Royal College and Director of Public Instruction are unique senior posts, not generic titles; two different J. Harwards holding both in succession on the bio's exact timeline is not a realistic hypothesis. The other 4 same-surname officials would have to occupy this single continuous staff-list line, which tracks one person through one career.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second J. Harward in the Ceylon education service in 1894-1915 (a separate staff-list line), or a record showing John Harward vacated the Principalship or Directorship before the staff-list run ended while a same-initial successor took over, or a forename for the staff-list entry other than John.

notes: Single-initial biography, so a strong independent dimension was required: satisfied by specific position matches on two unique senior posts plus the dated office redesignation (bio: Aug. 1913 change to dir. of educn.; staff list: department becomes 'Education Department' in 1914-1915). Stint years 1894-1915 sit entirely within the bio's attested career (1892 onward, still listed 1915).
